text.title: Stripe Webhook Handler Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Stripe Webhook Handler Pattern application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Verify Stripe-Signature header BEFORE any processing. Reject on mismatch with 400. Use Stripe SDK's verification helper.
rule.rationale: Without verification, webhook endpoint is a public DoS + injection surface; verification is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Use event.id as idempotency key. Reject duplicate events from prior delivery.
rule.rationale: Stripe retries on 5xx; without idempotency, duplicates re-process payments / fulfill orders multiple times.

rule.statement: Return 2xx within 5 seconds. Heavy work goes to a queue. Slow handlers cause Stripe retries + duplicates.
rule.rationale: Stripe times out at 30s; slow handlers create retry storms + duplicate processing.

rule.statement: Failed processing → dead-letter queue with manual review. Do NOT just 5xx and rely on Stripe retries (limited).
rule.rationale: Stripe retries 3 days then gives up; without DLQ, failed events are lost.

rule.statement: Handler is replay-safe: re-processing the same event produces the same outcome. State checked before mutation.
rule.rationale: Replay safety is the foundation of webhook reliability; without it, ops cannot safely re-deliver events.
